description:
To properly set the permissions of /var/log/apt/.*, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0644 /var/log/apt/.*
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/apt</tt> directory contains information about APT
and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /var/log/apt/.*, run the command: 
$ ls -l /var/log/apt/.*
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-r--r--
title: Verify Permissions on files in the /var/log/apt/.* directory
